4.', 'command': 'execution = tool.execute(query="Who brewed the first espresso coffee in space, Patrick Baudry or Samantha Cristoforetti?", add_citations=True)', 'result': ['Samantha Cristoforetti, an Italian astronaut, brewed the first espresso coffee in space on May 3, 2015.[1](https://www.theguardian.com/world/2015/may/04/space-italy-coffee-astronaut-espresso-cristoforetti), [2](https://www.guinnessworldrecords.com/world-records/414603-first-espresso-machine-in-space), [3](https://cogcoffee.com/cogblog/quest-for-perfect-space-coffee) She achieved this milestone aboard the International Space Station (ISS) using an experimental espresso machine named "ISSpresso," which was developed by the Italian coffee manufacturer Lavazza and the engineering firm Argotec, in collaboration with the Italian Space Agency (ASI).[1](https://www.theguardian.com/world/2015/may/04/space-italy-coffee-astronaut-espresso-cristoforetti), [4](https://www.sbs.com.au/news/article/italian-brews-first-espresso-in-space/i7yvv8s64), [2](https://www.guinnessworldrecords.com/world-records/414603-first-espresso-machine-in-space)

Cristoforetti's historic act involved her sipping the fresh brew from a specially designed zero-gravity cup and sharing a photo on Twitter, where she was wearing a Star Trek uniform.[2](https://www.guinnessworldrecords.com/world-records/414603-first-espresso-machine-in-space), [5](https://www.jpost.com/health-science/italian-astronaut-makes-first-espresso-in-space-releases-book-605233) This event was not only a moment of cultural significance but also served a scientific purpose, allowing researchers to study how hot fluids under high pressure behave in a zero-gravity environment.[2](https://www.guinnessworldrecords.com/world-records/414603-first-espresso-machine-in-space)

In contrast, Patrick Baudry was a French astronaut who flew aboard NASA's Space Shuttle mission STS-51-G in 1985, becoming the second French citizen in space.[6](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Patrick_Baudry), [7](https://www.spacelaunchschedule.com/astronaut/patrick-baudry/), [8](https://londonspeakerbureau.com/speaker-profile/patrick-baudry/) His missions focused on deploying communication satellites and conducting scientific experiments, but there is no record of him brewing espresso in space.[6](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Patrick_Baudry), [8](https://londonspeakerbureau.com/speaker-profile/patrick-baudry/)']}}

result:
Samantha Cristoforetti, an Italian astronaut, brewed the first espresso coffee in space on May 3, 2015.[1](https://www.theguardian.com/world/2015/may/04/space-italy-coffee-astronaut-espresso-cristoforetti), [2](https://www.guinnessworldrecords.com/world-records/414603-first-espresso-machine-in-space), [3](https://cogcoffee.com/cogblog/quest-for-perfect-space-coffee) She achieved this milestone aboard the International Space Station (ISS) using an experimental espresso machine named "ISSpresso," which was developed by the Italian coffee manufacturer Lavazza and the engineering firm Argotec, in collaboration with the Italian Space Agency (ASI).[1](https://www.theguardian.com/world/2015/may/04/space-italy-coffee-astronaut-espresso-cristoforetti), [4](https://www.sbs.com.au/news/article/italian-brews-first-espresso-in-space/i7yvv8s64), [2](https://www.guinnessworldrecords.com/world-records/414603-first-espresso-machine-in-space)

Cristoforetti's historic act involved her sipping the fresh brew from a specially designed zero-gravity cup and sharing a photo on Twitter, where she was wearing a Star Trek uniform.[2](https://www.guinnessworldrecords.com/world-records/414603-first-espresso-machine-in-space), [5](https://www.jpost.com/health-science/italian-astronaut-makes-first-espresso-in-space-releases-book-605233) This event was not only a moment of cultural significance but also served a scientific purpose, allowing researchers to study how hot fluids under high pressure behave in a zero-gravity environment.[2](https://www.guinnessworldrecords.com/world-records/414603-first-espresso-machine-in-space)

In contrast, Patrick Baudry was a French astronaut who flew aboard NASA's Space Shuttle mission STS-51-G in 1985, becoming the second French citizen in space.[6](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Patrick_Baudry), [7](https://www.spacelaunchschedule.com/astronaut/patrick-baudry/), [8](https://londonspeakerbureau.com/speaker-profile/patrick-baudry/) His missions focused on deploying communication satellites and conducting scientific experiments, but there is no record of him brewing espresso in space.[6](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Patrick_Baudry), [8](https://londonspeakerbureau.com/speaker-profile/patrick-baudry/)

direct_output:
<answer>
Samantha Cristoforetti brewed the first espresso coffee in space on May 3, 2015, aboard the International Space Station (ISS). This event was achieved using an experimental espresso machine named "ISSpresso," which was developed in collaboration with the Italian Space Agency (ASI). Patrick Baudry, a French astronaut, did not brew espresso in space.
</answer>
